sections.question: I am writing to request your opinion on a possible conflict of interest that I may have as a City Council Member for the City of Irvine. Currently before the City Council is a matter involving the McDonnell Douglas Realty Company and a Development Agreement for construction of a high-rise office building and monorail on McDonnell Douglas property known as Douglas Plaza. Although the only immediate problem is the one with McDonnell Douglas Realty, the possibility exists for future similar situations with Birtcher, The Irvine Company, and Sunwest. I would appreciate your opinion and recommended action.
sections.question_synthetic: A City Council Member seeks guidance on potential conflicts of interest arising from his company's security service contracts with McDonnell Douglas and other major builders/developers who have matters pending before the City Council.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Document shows clear facts regarding the requestor's position, company ownership, and contractual relationships with parties having business before the city council.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: I am writing to request your opinion on a possible conflict of interest that I may have as a City Council Member for the City of Irvine. Currently before the City Council is a matter involving the McDonnell Douglas Realty Company and a Development Agreement for construction of a high-rise office building and monorail on McDonnell Douglas property known as Douglas Plaza. Although the only immediate problem is the one with McDonnell Douglas Realty, the possibility exists for future similar situations with Birtcher, The Irvine Company, and Sunwest. I would appreciate your opinion and recommended action.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from Art Bloomier, a City Council Member for Irvine, seeking FPPC opinion on potential conflicts of interest related to his company's security contracts with McDonnell Douglas and other major developers having business before the City Council.
